div1.entryFree: Mortimer, name of a family allied to the royal house of England: H6A II, 5, 91. H6B IV, 2, 41. Edmund M. Earl of March, son-in-law of Lionel Duke of Clarence: H6B II, 2, 36. H6B II, 2, 36 IV, 2, 144. Roger M. Earl of March, his son: H6B II, 2, 37. H6C I, 1, 106. Edmund M., son of Roger, pretender to the crown under the reign of Henry IV: H4A I, 1, 38. I, 3, 80. I, 3, 80 I, 3, 80 I, 3, 80 I, 3, 80 II, 3, 84 etc. H6A II, 5, 2. H6A II, 5, 2 H6A II, 5, 2 H6B II, 2, 38. H6B II, 2, 38 Anne M., daughter of Roger, married to Richard of Cambridge: H6B II, 2, 38. H6B II, 2, 38 Eleanor M., her sister: H6B II, 2, 38. John M.: H6B III, 1, 359. H6B III, 1, 359 Sir John and Sir Hugh M. H6C I, 2, 62. Lord M. of Scotland, called so by mistake, Lord March of Scotland being meant: H4A III, 2, 164.
